county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Riley County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Manhattan. Named for General Bennett C. Riley. Fort Riley (1853) military reservation. Kansas State University. Swedish and German settlers. Blue River and Kansas River confluence.
county_metadata.research_tips: Riley County Register of Deeds in Manhattan holds land records from 1855. Manhattan is home to Kansas State University -- KSU Special Collections and Archives hold local history materials. Fort Riley (established 1853) military records at NARA predate Kansas Territory. The Riley County Historical Society and Museum in Manhattan has excellent local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Strong Swedish and German immigrant communities left church records from the 1850s-1860s. Probate Court records from 1855. Manhattan newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Riley County records. The Big Blue River and Kansas River confluence was the settlement center.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Riley County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Manhattan

source_profiles.source_name: Riley County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Riley County Register of Deeds, Manhattan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Riley County from 1855; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

source_profiles.source_name: Riley County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Riley County Probate Court, Manhattan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Riley County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.repository: NARA / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Military service records, pension files, muster rolls for Riley County residents; Fort Riley post records at NARA; Kansas adjutant general records at KSHS; Civil War, Indian Wars, Spanish-American War, and WWI/WWII records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rooks County formed 1872, organized 1877. County seat is Stockton. Named for Private John C. Rooks, killed in Civil War. Northwestern Kansas. Solomon River valley. Czech, German, and Scandinavian settlers. Oil production from the early 1900s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rooks County Register of Deeds in Stockton holds land records from the 1870s. The Rooks County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Czech, German, and Scandinavian settlers left church records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Stockton newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Rooks County records. The Solomon River valley was the primary settlement corridor. The county had significant oil production from the early 1900s; petroleum company records may document workers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Rooks County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Stockton

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1872s-1882s

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rush County formed 1874, organized 1876. County seat is La Crosse. Named for Captain Alexander Rush, killed in Civil War. Post Rock Limestone country. German-Russian Catholic settlers. Barbed wire collection at the museum. Central-western Kansas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rush County Register of Deeds in La Crosse holds land records from 1874. The Rush County Historical Society and Museum in La Crosse has local collections including the famous barbed wire collection. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. German-Russian Catholic settlers left parish records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from 1874. La Crosse newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county is in the Post Rock Limestone region. Oil production from the mid-20th century. District Court naturalization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Rush County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in La Crosse

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1874s-1884s

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Russell County formed 1867, organized 1871. County seat is Russell. Named for Captain Avra P. Russell, killed in Civil War. Hometown of US Senator Bob Dole. Volga German settlers. Oil production. Wilson Lake area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Russell County Register of Deeds in Russell holds land records from the 1870s. Russell was the hometown of US Senator Bob Dole -- the Dole Institute at KU has some local history. The Russell County Historical Society and Fossil Creek Museum has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. German-Russian Volga German settlers left Catholic and Lutheran church records. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Russell newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Russell County records. Oil production from the early 1900s brought workers. Wilson Lake construction affected some communities; check KSHS for relocation records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Russell County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Russell

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Saline County formed 1860. County seat is Salina. Named for the Saline River. Major commercial center in central Kansas. Swedish, German, and English settlers. Kansas Wesleyan University. Smoky Hill Trail corridor. Schilling Air Force Base (closed 1965).
county_metadata.research_tips: Saline County Register of Deeds in Salina holds land records from the 1860s. Salina is a major commercial center in central Kansas. The Smoky Hill Museum (Saline County Historical Society) in Salina has excellent local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Swedish, German, and English settlers left church records from the 1860s. Kansas Wesleyan University and Marymount College archives hold local church records. Probate Court records from the 1860s. Salina Journal newspaper at KSHS and Newspapers.com. FamilySearch has microfilmed Saline County records. Schilling Air Force Base (later Salina Municipal Airport) military records at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Saline County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Salina

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Scott County formed 1873, organized 1886. County seat is Scott City. Named for General Winfield Scott. Western Kansas county. El Quartelejo Pueblo ruins (northernmost pueblo in North America). Lake Scott State Park. Sparsely populated agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Scott County Register of Deeds in Scott City holds land records from the 1880s. A western Kansas county with late settlement. The Scott County Historical Society and El Quartelejo Museum in Scott City has local collections documenting both Native American and settlement history. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations. Scott City newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Lake Scott State Park has Pueblo ruins (El Quartelejo) -- the northernmost pueblo in North America.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Scott County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Scott City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sedgwick County formed 1867 from Butler County. County seat is Wichita, Kansas's largest city. Named for Major General John Sedgwick, killed in Civil War. Wichita was a cattle town before becoming the 'Air Capital of the World' (Boeing/Spirit, Cessna, Beechcraft). Diverse population. McConnell Air Force Base.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sedgwick County Register of Deeds in Wichita holds land records from 1867. Wichita is Kansas's largest city and was a famous cattle town before becoming an aircraft manufacturing center. The Wichita-Sedgwick County Historical Museum has excellent genealogical collections. The Wichita Public Library's Local History section has extensive resources. BLM GLO land patents. Diverse immigrant communities (German-Russian, Mexican, Vietnamese, African American) left varied church records. Probate Court records from 1867. Wichita Eagle newspaper at KSHS and Newspapers.com (from 1872). FamilySearch has microfilmed Sedgwick County records. Aircraft industry records (Boeing/Spirit, Cessna, Beechcraft) may document workers. McConnell Air Force Base records at NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Sedgwick County Register of Deeds online index; historical deed books at county office and KSHS microfilm

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1867; deed books are substantially complete

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Seward County formed 1886, organized 1886. County seat is Liberal. Named for William H. Seward, Secretary of State. Southwestern Kansas. Natural gas production and meatpacking. Diverse modern population. Dust Bowl effects. Land of Oz celebration.
county_metadata.research_tips: Seward County Register of Deeds in Liberal holds land records from 1886. Liberal is in the far-southwestern part of the state. The Seward County Historical Society and Mid-America Air Museum in Liberal has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from 1886. Church records from pioneer congregations. Liberal newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Natural gas production and meatpacking brought diverse communities. Mexican American and Southeast Asian immigrant communities from the late 20th century. Dust Bowl effects were significant.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Seward County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Liberal

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Shawnee County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Topeka, the state capital. Named for the Shawnee people. Home of Kansas State Historical Society (KSHS), the central genealogical repository. Brown v. Board of Education originated here. Exoduster community. Free State movement center.
county_metadata.research_tips: Shawnee County Register of Deeds in Topeka holds land records from 1855. Topeka is the state capital and home to the Kansas State Historical Society (KSHS), which is the central repository for Kansas genealogical research. The Kansas State Library and the Topeka Genealogical Society provide additional resources. BLM GLO land patents. The Brown v. Board of Education National Historic Site documents African American history. Exoduster community records are at KSHS. Church records from diverse denominations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. Topeka Capital-Journal newspaper at KSHS and Newspapers.com. FamilySearch has microfilmed Shawnee County records. District Court naturalization records. The Shawnee and Potawatomi presence predates territorial settlement -- tribal records at NARA Kansas City.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Shawnee County Register of Deeds records; historical deed books at county office and KSHS microfilm

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sheridan County formed 1873, organized 1880. County seat is Hoxie. Named for General Philip Sheridan. Northwestern Kansas. Late settlement through homesteading. Dust Bowl effects. German-Russian and Bohemian settlers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sheridan County Register of Deeds in Hoxie holds land records from the 1880s. A northwestern Kansas county. The Sheridan County Historical Society and Museum in Hoxie has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Catholic and Methodist congregations. Hoxie Sentinel newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl effects caused outmigration. German-Russian and Bohemian settlers left church records at local parishes.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Sheridan County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hoxie

source_profiles.source_name: Sheridan County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Sheridan County Register of Deeds, Hoxie
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Sheridan County from 1873; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1873s-1883s

source_profiles.source_name: Sheridan County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Sheridan County Probate Court, Hoxie
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Sheridan County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.source_name: Sheridan County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1880-1950 for Sheridan County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Sheridan County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Sheridan County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Sheridan County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Sheridan County Historic Newspapers -- KSHS
source_profiles.repository: Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Sheridan County towns at KSHS on microfilm; legal notices, homestead proof notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, and community news; essential for documenting early settlement in western Kansas

source_profiles.source_name: Sheridan County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Sheridan County Probate Court, Hoxie / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Sheridan County from 1873; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911
